skill1.kitLine: ■ Activates when performing a Full Charge attack. Affects self.
skill1.note: Trigger header for the Pierce line; listed unmodeled. Inert anyway (Pierce/pierceDamagePct v1-inert).
Inert: 'Gain Pierce' is v1-inert per note; no block, no hasPierce.

skill1.kitLine: ■ Activates when not in the Embarrassment state and when Full Charge lasts for 0.5 sec or more. Affects self.
skill1.note: The 0.5s-charge-hold trigger is a manual action the AI never performs; approximated by the mode split. Default auto mode = never active (plain SR); manual mode = treated as permanent. A naive block-only check misses this gating.

skill1.kitLine: Embarrassment — Function: forcefully reloads at reduced reload speed, increases attack capabilities, deals Distributed Damage.
skill1.note: Summary line realized via reloadSpeedPct -50 + atkPct 118.7 + the shotFired 290% distributed block. Only in manual mode.

skill1.kitLine: Effect 1: Affects all enemies. Deals 290% of final ATK as Distributed Damage.
skill1.where: skill1[1] shotFired flatDamage atkPct 290 flavor distributed (mode: manual)
skill1.note: Fires ~once/cycle because ammo floored to 1. Manual mode only.

skill1.kitLine: Effect 2: Affects self. Removes 100% of ammo.
skill1.note: Approximation: floors magazine to 1 → one shot then reload. First magazine still fires all 6 starting rounds before the cap applies (early-fight over-count, see note uncertainty 2).

skill1.kitLine: Effect 3: Affects self. Reload speed is fixed at a 50% reduction for 1 reload(s).
skill1.note: Modeled as permanent rather than 1-reload; re-triggers every cycle so effectively always on.
No discrete forced-reload block; captured implicitly by the 1-shot-then-reload cadence from the ammo floor.

skill1.kitLine: Effect 5: Affects self. ATK ▲ 118.7% for 40 sec.
skill1.note: Modeled as permanent (refreshed every cycle) rather than a 40s duration.

skill2.kitLine: ■ Activates only when in Embarrassment status. Affects self.
skill2.note: Trigger header for the Pierce Damage line; listed unmodeled. Inert (pierceDamagePct v1-inert).
Inert: pierceDamagePct is v1-inert per note; deliberately not blocked.

skill2.kitLine: ■ Activates only when in Overconfident, Huh?! status. Affects all enemies every 2 sec.
skill2.note: Trigger is the Overconfident status granted by burst; proxied as burstCast. NOT mode-gated → fires in both auto and manual modes. Parser had dropped it under an unsupported trigger; re-added as DoT (5 ticks).

skill2.kitLine: Deals 447.7% of final ATK as Distributed Damage.
skill2.note: Distributed flavor lost (dot has no flavor field) but vs single boss distributed deals full value and she has no distributedDamagePct, so no boost lost.

burst.note: Header; burst block targets self. Not mode-gated → applies in both modes.
Unparsed status-name line. Functionally benign: the DoT this status gates (S2 447.7%/2s) IS modeled via the burstCast DoT.

burst.kitLine: Gains Immunity to Embarrassment for 10 sec.
burst.note: NOT modeled. During the 10s burst she should revert to a full 6-round magazine + normal reload (more charged normals, no 290% procs); instead she keeps the 1-shot slow-reload cadence → burst-window normals UNDER-counted, ~1-2 stray 290% procs OVER-counted (partial offset). Counter-buff approach rejected (would re-proc 290% on every burst normal — larger over-count).
⚑ DEAD BLOCK (kit-status F3): present in the array but never fires — no hasPierce config and pierceDamagePct is v1-inert. modeled != working. Auto-filled from offline parser; not hand-verified.

Present in the burst block (auto-filled from offline parser, behavior-identical to prior runtime parse). Note text says 'left to the parser (correct)'; either way it is represented in a block and is functional.

documentedConfounds.text: F3 dead block: burst pierceDamagePct 117.64 never fires (no hasPierce; pierceDamagePct v1-inert). modeled != working; finding says 'Fix hasPierce'.
documentedConfounds.why: A pierce-damage buff that should apply during the 10s burst window contributes nothing → sim under-counts damage, pushing the ratio down (aligns with observed COLD 0.68).

documentedConfounds.text: F3/auto-mode: default 'auto (no Embarrassment)' mode is too pessimistic — real auto-play procs Embarrassment partially, but the sim models her as a plain SR with no 118.7% ATK / no 290% procs / normal magazine.
documentedConfounds.why: Likely the dominant confound: board runs full-auto, so the sim drops the entire Embarrassment damage package that real auto-play partially realizes → large under-count → COLD.

documentedConfounds.text: F5: the old 'poor auto-play' 0.70 ACCEPTED-DECISION rationale is DIRECTION-SUSPECT — it predates the ratio-direction fix; poor auto-play predicts a HOT sim, but she is observed COLD.
documentedConfounds.why: Interpretive, not a mechanic: it undermines the framing of the 0.68 residual as a benign accepted decision and suggests it may instead be a genuine under-count (reinforces the two COLD confounds above).

documentedConfounds.text: Uncertainty 1: burst 'Immunity to Embarrassment for 10s' not modeled — she keeps the 1-shot slow-reload cadence during burst instead of reverting to a full 6-round magazine + normal reload.
documentedConfounds.why: Net under-count of burst-window charged normals (partially offset by ~1-2 stray 290% procs that are over-counted) → pushes sim low.

documentedConfounds.text: Uncertainty 2: first magazine fires all 6 starting rounds before the -100 max-ammo cap applies on reload.
documentedConfounds.why: Minor early-fight OVER-count of normals + 290% procs → pushes sim high, i.e. against the observed COLD gap (not a cause of it).

documentedConfounds.text: Uncertainty 3: S2 DoT loses the 'distributed' flavor (dot has no flavor field).
documentedConfounds.why: No boost lost: distributed vs a single boss deals full value and she carries no distributedDamagePct.
Status-name line only; the damage it gates (S2 447.7%/2s DoT) is modeled via the burstCast DoT.

documentedConfounds.text: burst: unparsed effect "Gains Immunity to Embarrassment for 10 sec."
documentedConfounds.why: Same substantive gap as note uncertainty 1 — the unmodeled immunity causes a net burst-window under-count.

authorIntentSummary: The author models Milk: Blooming Bunny with two modes: a default 'auto (no Embarrassment)' mode (matching full-auto validation runs) where she is a plain SR keeping only S2's burst DoT (447.7%/2s for 10s) and the burst buffs, and a 'manual (Embarrassment cycle)' mode that treats Embarrassment as a permanent operating state — passive maxAmmoPct -100 (1-shot/reload cadence), reloadSpeedPct -50, permanent ATK +118.7%, and a shotFired 290% distributed proc. Key approximations: the 0.5s-charge-hold Embarrassment trigger is encoded as the mode split rather than a real trigger; the burst's 10s Embarrassment-immunity is deliberately NOT modeled (under-counts burst-window normals, partially offset by stray procs); the first magazine over-counts 6 starting rounds; and all Pierce buffs (S1 Gain Pierce, S2 +64.7%, burst +117.64%) are inert — the burst pierce block is a documented dead block (no hasPierce, pierceDamagePct v1-inert). The known-not-faithful spot is the COLD 0.68 residual, with the documented suspicion that the default auto mode is too pessimistic (real auto-play procs Embarrassment partially) and that the older 'poor auto-play' accepted-decision rationale is direction-suspect.
